title: Neon Drift
description: A top-down cyberpunk racing game with procedurally generated tracks, neon aesthetics, and pulse-pounding synthwave soundtrack.

devlogs.entry: Added neon shader effects and particle trails. Performance optimised to 60fps on mid-range hardware. The glow pass was the trickiest part — had to write a custom screen-space blur.
Initial prototype complete. Core driving mechanics feel solid with responsive steering and drift physics. Working on procedural track generation next.
Project kickoff. Settled on Godot 4 for its new rendering pipeline. Sketched out the core game loop: race, drift, score, repeat.

title: Market Mayhem
description: A strategy game where you manage a trading firm through market crashes, bull runs, and black swan events. Every decision has cascading consequences.

devlogs.entry: First playable build is live internally. The economic simulation loop is surprisingly fun — testers kept playing 'just one more quarter'.
Implemented the event system. Black swan events now fire randomly with weighted probability. Market sentiment shifts feel organic.
Concept phase complete. Designing the core economic simulation loop. Inspired by real trading experience.

title: Void Protocol
description: A minimalist puzzle game set in deep space. Hack satellite networks, reroute power grids, and uncover a conspiracy hidden in corrupted data streams.

devlogs.entry: Puzzle set 2 complete — 12 new levels with branching solutions. Added a hint system that nudges without spoiling.
Switched to Phaser 3. The scene management and tween system saved weeks of work. First 5 puzzles are fully playable.
Started Void Protocol as a game jam entry. The core mechanic — rotating hexagonal nodes to complete circuits — clicked immediately.
